product: AI-powered operating system for real estate investors
deliverable: Intelligence Center — AI workflow execution console (v1 MVP)
target_platform: GoHighLevel (marketplace app or iframe custom menu page)
posting_summary: Full-Stack SaaS MVP: a lean, functional Intelligence Center dashboard that proves the core workflow (Run → Generate → Review → Revise → Approve → Save/Push) across 3 primary AI engines. Not a chatbot, not analytics. An execution console where users run engines, review outputs, request edits, and gate approvals before saving or pushing to external destinations.

core_workflow.description: The non-negotiable loop every engine must support
steps: Run engine (with user input + persistent intelligence)
Save or Push to destination (only after approval)

authority_engine.description: Generates founder-authority content (LinkedIn posts, X/Twitter threads, thought-leadership pieces)
authority_engine.io: User input (topic/angle/tone) → AI-generated drafts → Review → Revise → Approve → Save as draft or push to LinkedIn/X

newsletter_engine.description: Generates newsletter content + metadata (subject line options, preview text, body copy)
newsletter_engine.io: User input (topic/audience) → AI generates body + 3 subject-line options + preview text → Review → Revise → Approve → Save as draft or schedule/send

knowledge_engine_lite.description: Generates or curates core business knowledgebase (customer-facing, internal playbooks, processes). Stores to Google Drive or GitHub.
knowledge_engine_lite.io: User input (business context/topic) → AI generates KB entry → Review → Approve → Save to Google Drive or GitHub
minimal_features: Destination picker (Google Drive or GitHub folder)
Save with receipt + link to saved asset
knowledge_engine_lite.note: Full analytics automation is NOT required for V1. Stub is acceptable; focus on the save workflow.

intel_brief_stub.description: A lite/manual/stubbed section showing intended structure with placeholder items
intel_brief_stub.note: Intentionally static/demo data. Full analytics automation is not required for V1.

outputs_pending_review_queue.description: List of generated outputs awaiting user approval or revision
minimal_features: Show pending outputs by engine + timestamp

prompt_chips.description: Quick-start command chips for common tasks
prompt_chips.example: [Generate LinkedIn Post] [Create Newsletter] [Document Process] [Brainstorm Topic Ideas]
prompt_chips.behavior: Click chip → pre-fill engine input with smart defaults

persistent_intelligence_input.description: Always-present command/input bar at the console's top or side
persistent_intelligence_input.behavior: Global text input for persistent user context (e.g., 'Operating in Austin market, selling fix-and-flip deals, lead source is cold calls'). Engines can reference this context when generating outputs.

features: Left engine rail (3 engine buttons/cards to switch context)
Right panel: Outputs Pending Review queue + Intel Brief stub
Bottom or side: Run history, usage/cost log, error messages

backend_architecture_required.note: Must be REPRESENTED / architecturally demonstrated in the demo. In a full production system, these live on the real Pathwaize backend. The demo models them in-browser via WebGPU + IndexedDB.
components: Engine orchestrator (routes requests to the correct engine pipeline)
Backend-based LLM routing layer (frontend never calls OpenAI/Claude/any provider directly; it talks to a router that selects the model/provider)
Prompt/template management (store + versioned prompts for each engine)
Engine run records (log every run: timestamp, engine, input, model, output, cost)
Output storage (persist generated outputs with metadata)
Output status tracking (pending / generating / needs-review / revised / approved / pushed)
Review and approval workflow (user can reject, request edits, or approve)
Revision workflow (generate alternative outputs or refined versions)
Usage and cost logging (track API costs per user/workspace/engine, visible in dashboard)
Error handling (graceful failure messages, retry logic if applicable)
Google Drive connection (OAuth + ability to save KB entries to Drive)
GitHub connection (OAuth + ability to save KB entries to GitHub repos)
Subaccount/workspace association (multi-tenant: each workspace has its own engine settings, run history, API quotas)

llm_routing_requirement.critical: Frontend MUST NOT call OpenAI, Claude, or any LLM provider directly.
llm_routing_requirement.contract: Frontend → Backend router. Backend router handles: prompt execution, model/provider selection, permissions, logging, usage/cost tracking, run history, error handling, approval-gated actions.
llm_routing_requirement.provider_agnostic_proof: System must be structured so additional providers or models can be added without rebuilding the product. Start with one primary provider in V1.
example_backend_routed_actions: Preparing approved outputs to be pushed or saved

approval_gating_requirement.critical: LLM-generated outputs NEVER automatically publish, send, or update critical systems without explicit user approval.
must_demonstrate: A pending output cannot push to LinkedIn/X without user approval click
A newsletter cannot be scheduled/sent without approval click
A KB entry cannot be saved to Drive/GitHub without approval click
Revision requests block the push path until resolved

requirements_for_cover_letter.note: The client's posting includes 8 specific 'TO APPLY' questions. Cover letter must answer ALL 8 explicitly and reference the live demo by name + link.

questions.question: Examples of SaaS dashboards, AI apps, or workflow tools you have built
questions.note: Link to live demo + 2-3 portfolio references

questions.question: Your recommended tech stack for this MVP (currently using AWS and Supabase)
questions.note: Address their existing infra; recommend a cohesive stack that respects AWS + Supabase

questions.question: Your experience with LLM-backed workflows
questions.note: Concrete examples of apps that integrated LLMs; mention backend routing, cost tracking, error handling

questions.question: Your experience with GoHighLevel integrations
questions.note: Address marketplace app vs iframe custom menu link; SSO/subaccount context; sizing + postMessage

questions.question: How you would structure the backend LLM routing layer so the product is not hard-coded to one model or provider
questions.note: Explain provider-agnostic contract + config-driven model selection

questions.question: How you would prevent approval-gated actions from publishing, pushing, or updating connected systems without user approval
questions.note: Explain workflow gates + status tracking + why outputs don't auto-publish

questions.question: How you would approach building this as a lean MVP without overengineering it
questions.note: Scope-down: focus on 3 engines + core workflow loop, skip advanced analytics/continuous KB monitoring/proactive gap detection

questions.question: Your estimated timeline and fixed-price or milestone-based budget range
questions.note: Give a ballpark range; structure as milestones (UI shell, Authority Engine, Newsletter Engine, KB Engine, Review/Approval flow, Polish/Deploy)

requirements_for_demo.note: The evaluator will grade the demo against these explicit gates. Every one must be visible, functional, or clearly modeled.
gates: 3 engines (Authority, Newsletter, Knowledge Lite) each wired end-to-end through run/generate/review/revise/approve/save-push
Intel Brief stub with its 6 named sections (What changed, What's working, What's leaking, What needs action, Recommended decisions, Priority list)
Prompt chips (quick-start command suggestions that pre-fill engine input)
Backend LLM routing layer story (literally shown via an in-browser LLMRouter module with same request/response contract as production backend)
Approval-gating: LLM outputs NEVER auto-publish/push/save; user must explicitly click 'Approve' before a Push action succeeds
Google Drive + GitHub destination picker for Knowledge Engine KB saves
Run records visible (history log showing: timestamp, engine, input, model used, output, cost, status)
Output status tracking (pending / generating / needs-review / revised / approved / pushed — shown as colored chips)
Usage and cost logging (visible somewhere in the dashboard: total calls, total cost, per-engine breakdown, cost per run)
Error handling (graceful error messages when generation fails or user input is invalid)
Subaccount/workspace association (workspace selector or multi-tenant context indicator)
Design is bespoke + professional (AI execution console aesthetic, NOT michaelwegter.com's portfolio look, NOT a generic chatbot)
No em/en dashes anywhere in the UI or proposal text

do_not_build: Advanced analytics, continuous KB monitoring, proactive gap detection, or future engines beyond placeholder stubs
Real OpenAI/Claude API keys exposed in the demo (WebGPU local models eliminate this risk + cost)
More than ~12 files in the demo codebase
Build time > 3 minutes (WebLLM model loads from CDN at runtime, not bundled)
More than 1 hero feature + 2 supporting features
Over-engineering the Intel Brief (it's intentionally a lightweight, demo-data stub)
what_can_be_simulated: Save/push to Google Drive and GitHub (show the file that WOULD be written, target path, and success receipt in-browser; real GitHub push only if planner greenlights a backend blueprint)
LinkedIn/X post scheduling/push (show mock success response)
Newsletter delivery metadata (show when a newsletter would be sent; do not actually send emails)

design_direction.theme: AI-powered execution console for real-estate investors
design_direction.aesthetic: Command deck / mission-control: dark, dense, professional SaaS. NOT playful, NOT a generic chatbot bubble UI.
suggested_elements: Deep charcoal/near-black canvas with a single confident accent (Pathwaize brand color if found; else fintech blue/teal or authority gold)
Panel-based layout: left engine rail, center run/review workspace, right queue + Intel Brief
Typography: crisp grotesk/Inter-class UI face + mono for run IDs, logs, cost figures
Status system: pending / generating / needs-review / revised / approved / pushed — colored state chips everywhere
Dense but scannable: many status indicators at a glance, but not overwhelming
design_direction.who_finalizes: planner (lock palette hex + fonts after researcher gathers Pathwaize brand + GoHighLevel context)

tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.note: Proposal must address their existing AWS + Supabase infrastructure explicitly.
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.frontend: Next.js / React embedded in GoHighLevel via iframe custom menu page
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.backend: Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Row-Level Security for workspace/user isolation) for run records, outputs, status, logs; a dedicated LLM routing service (Node/Next.js API routes or a small service on AWS Lambda + API Gateway) exposing one provider-agnostic /route contract
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.auth: Supabase Auth (with OAuth for Google + GitHub for Drive/GitHub KB connections)
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.integrations: Google Drive API + GitHub API, both via OAuth server-side (backend handles tokens, frontend never sees keys)
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.llm_provider: Start with one (OpenAI or Claude); structure so adding another is a config + provider-class addition, not a rebuild
tech_stack_recommendation_for_client.rationale: Respects their AWS + Supabase investments; provider-agnostic routing scales to multi-provider later; server-side OAuth keeps keys secure; Supabase RLS handles workspace isolation elegantly.

demo_implementation_note.approach: WebGPU + Transformers.js for the LLM (client-side, 4-bit quantized small model like Qwen2.5-1.5B or Llama-3-8B-Instruct-Q4). Browser IndexedDB for run records + output storage + logs. In-browser LLMRouter module mirrors the backend routing contract exactly (swappable at config time). This proves the architecture without requiring a real backend for demo reviewers, while staying 100% honest about the production flow (frontend talks to a routing layer, not directly to OpenAI).
webgpu_best_practices_from_brief: Prompt chaining / routing over free-loop ReAct (avoid hallucinations from small models)
Enforced structured (JSON-schema / grammar) outputs (100% syntactic correctness)
IndexedDB for run records / output storage / logs (persistent + performant)
Sliding context window (cap conversation to last 4-6 turns + async summarization)
Local self-correction / reflection (validate output format in JS; retry once if it fails)

definition_of_done: All 8 'TO APPLY' cover-letter questions answered explicitly + demo link referenced twice
Demo builds and loads with no console errors on first paint
All 3 engines (Authority, Newsletter, Knowledge Lite) wired end-to-end and visibly working
Approval-gating prevents unapproved outputs from pushing; user must click 'Approve' first
Run records + output status tracking + usage/cost logging all visible in the UI
Intel Brief stub with all 6 sections present (even if demo data)
Outputs Pending Review queue functional (can select, approve, request edits, push)
Prompt chips and persistent intelligence input present and functional
Design is bespoke to real-estate/fintech vertical (NOT portfolio look), professional, no em/en dashes
Demo URL resolves live after deploy; live /work-samples/:slug route returns 200

priority_build_order_from_posting: 1. UI shell (console layout, NOT chatbot, NOT analytics)
2. Authority Engine end-to-end (run → generate → review → revise → approve → save/push)
4. Knowledge Engine Lite (generate + KB storage destination picker)

handoff.produced: Structured brief.json capturing: client (Pathwaize) + deliverable (Intelligence Center MVP), core workflow loop, 3 engines (Authority/Newsletter/Knowledge Lite) with minimal feature sets, supporting UI (Intel Brief stub, Outputs queue, prompt chips, persistent input, console shell), full backend architecture requirements (LLM routing, approval gating, storage, multi-tenant, integrations), the 8 'TO APPLY' cover-letter questions + explicit answers required, demo validation gates (all 13 requirements graded by evaluator), scope fences (no analytics/overengineering), bespoke design direction, recommended tech stack addressing client's AWS + Supabase infra, priority build order from posting, success criteria (definition of done).
handoff.decisions: Feature richness 10 = full functional demo (WebGPU local LLM behind in-browser LLMRouter module mirrors production backend contract exactly; provider-agnostic = swappable at config). Hero = end-to-end run/review/approve/push on Authority + Newsletter; Supporting = Knowledge Lite + console shell. Build order strict from posting. Design must be bespoke execution-console aesthetic (dark, professional, NOT portfolio look). Cover letter must answer all 8 TO APPLY questions + reference live demo twice. No em/en dashes anywhere.
handoff.next_needs: Planner: lock bespoke palette (hex) + typography + layout from this brief. Decide whether a real mw-backend GitHub blueprint is warranted for save/push (default = simulate in-browser convincingly, greenlight real blueprint only if planner/CEO agree). Researcher: find Pathwaize's real brand (site, logo, colors) + GoHighLevel embed specifics + competitor workflow UIs (Jasper, Copy.ai). CEO: validate that the WebGPU approach + in-browser LLMRouter genuinely proves the 'provider-agnostic' requirement without misrepresenting the architecture.
handoff.risks: WebGPU availability varies by browser (demo must gracefully detect + show fallback option; ideally mock-LLM path so non-WebGPU reviewers still see the workflow). Model download latency on first load (pick smallest 4-bit quantized model; show load progress UI). Reconciling 'frontend must not call LLM' with client-side model must be framed honestly (in demo, router swaps to WebGPU provider; in production, identical interface calls backend; this is the architectural demonstration, not a hack).
